item.title: Cuba Seeks Russian Drones, Eyes Guantanamo Attack
item.description: US intelligence reports Cuba has purchased 300+ Russian and Iranian drones and is discussing attacks on Guantanamo Bay and Key West, with Iranian military advisors now in Havana.

item.title: Turkish Arms Subsidiary Rebrands After Israel Sales Backlash
item.description: Repkon USA, a Turkish-owned defense contractor, rebranded to Paligen Technologies after revelations it would supply bomb bodies for Israel, sparking protests in Istanbul and parliamentary inquiries in Turkey.

item.title: Ethiopia-Eritrea War Fears as Tigray Tensions Escalate
item.description: TPLF leader Debretsion Gebremichael reclaimed presidency of Tigray region, challenging the interim administration. Ethiopian fighter jets seen over Mekelle as fears grow of renewed conflict with Eritrea.

item.title: Zelensky Touts Shift in Battlefield Balance After Moscow Strike
item.description: Following Ukraines largest drone attack on Moscow in over a year, Zelensky announced a shift in momentum and promised more deep strikes as Russia downed over 3,000 drones in the past week.

item.title: Hungary Opposition Leads Orbán for First Time in 15 Years
item.description: Péter Magyars Tisza party leads Viktor Orbáns Fidesz by 12 points ahead of April elections in what Politico calls the EUs most consequential election of 2026.

item.title: Drone Strikes UAE Nuclear Plant as US and Iran Signal Readiness to Resume War
item.description: A drone attack targeted the Barakah nuclear power plant in Abu Dhabi, with UAE authorities blaming Iran or its proxies. The incident comes as both the US and Iran have signaled willingness to return to negotiations amid heightened tensions in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: WHO Declares Global Health Emergency Over Ebola Outbreak in Congo and Uganda
item.description: The World Health Organization has declared an international health emergency following an Ebola outbreak spreading across Congo and Uganda. The outbreak has already killed 15 people and raised concerns about regional containment.

item.title: Senate Parliamentarian Blocks $1 Billion White House Security Proposal
item.description: The Senate parliamentarian ruled that a $1 billion proposal for White House security funding, including a proposed ballroom, cannot proceed under budget reconciliation rules. The decision deals a blow to administration spending plans.

item.title: Climate Change Reducing Oxygen Levels in Rivers Worldwide, Scientists Find
item.description: A new study reveals that global warming is systematically reducing dissolved oxygen in rivers across the globe, creating expanding "dead zones" that threaten aquatic ecosystems and drinking water supplies.

item.title: China economic growth stalls as output and retail sales miss forecasts
item.description: Industrial output and retail sales sharply undershot expectations in April as the world second-biggest economy wrestled with higher energy costs from the Iran war and persistently weak domestic demand.

item.title: Oil prices climb after drone attack on UAE nuclear plant
item.description: Oil prices extended gains as efforts to end the US-Iran conflict appeared stalled following a drone attack on a UAE nuclear power plant and President Trump prepared to discuss military options on Iran.

item.title: German prosecutors expose Russian sanctions-busting network supplying military tech
item.description: A four-year investigation uncovered a Lübeck-based trading firm that routed dual-use components through Turkey into Russia, violating Western sanctions to supply the Kremlin war machine.

item.title: Brent crude breaks $111 as Gulf drone attacks and Hormuz fears spark shortage concerns
item.description: Oil prices surged in early Asian trading after drone strikes on UAE and Saudi Arabia, combined with failed Iran diplomacy, raised fears of global supply disruptions. WTI hit $108.20 while Brent climbed to $111.50.

item.title: Xi and Trump agree Iran must not obtain nuclear weapons in strategic shift on Gulf security
item.description: China and the US reached a rare point of agreement on Iran policy during Trumps China visit, with both leaders signaling that Tehrans nuclear ambitions must be contained, reshaping Middle East power dynamics.

item.title: UAE Blames Iran for Drone Strike Near Barakah Nuclear Power Plant
item.description: The United Arab Emirates accused Iran or its proxies of launching a drone that sparked a fire just outside the Barakah nuclear facility. No radiation leaks were reported, but the incident marks a dangerous escalation as Iran war ceasefire talks remain stalled.

item.title: NATO Defense Ministers Meet Without US Leadership Amid Questions
item.description: European allies downplayed concerns after US Defense Secretary Hegseth skipped the NATO gathering in Brussels, marking the second consecutive high-level US absence. Germany and Iceland insisted the alliance remains strong despite the unusual US no-shows.

item.title: G7 Finance Chiefs Convene in Paris to Tackle Trade Imbalances
item.description: Finance ministers from G7 nations meet in Paris to coordinate critical raw material supplies and address global economic tensions amid geopolitical differences threatening group cohesion.

item.title: Drone Strike Hits UAE Barakah Nuclear Plant Amid Iran War Deadlock
item.description: A drone strike caused a fire at the UAE sole nuclear power plant, with Emirati officials investigating the source and vowing response to what they called a terrorist attack.

item.title: Israel to Establish Defense Offices in Former UNRWA East Jerusalem Compound
item.description: Israel plans to set up defense offices in the former UNRWA compound in East Jerusalem after demolishing structures, a move the UN agency called a violation of international law.

item.title: Russia Destroyed Over 3,000 Ukrainian Drones in Past Week
item.description: Russian defense ministry reports intercepting and destroying at least 3,124 Ukrainian drones over the past week, as Ukraine continues its drone campaign against Russian targets.

item.title: UAE Pivots from Oil to AI as Post-War Economic Strategy
item.description: With its OPEC era over, the UAE is betting big on artificial intelligence to build economic bridges to the Global South, bolstered by cutting-edge Nvidia chip deliveries. The strategy has been tested by Iranian attacks on Emirati data centers during the US-Iran conflict.

item.title: Kremlin Signals Readiness to Resume Dialogue with Europe on Ukraine War
item.description: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov said Moscow is open to restarting dialogue with Europe over the war in Ukraine, following a major Ukrainian drone attack on the Moscow region. The comments come as European officials debate future engagement with Moscow amid uncertainty over US peace efforts.

item.title: Philippine VP Sara Duterte Faces Senate Impeachment Trial
item.description: Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte faces a senate impeachment trial on charges including bribery, corruption, and threatening the president's life. The outcome could reshape the 2028 presidential race and determine the Duterte clan's future in national politics.

item.title: G7 Finance Ministers Meet in Paris Amid Trade Tensions and Iran War Fallout
item.description: Finance ministers from the Group of Seven nations convene in Paris to address global economic imbalances and coordinate critical raw material supplies. Geopolitical differences over the Iran conflict threaten to test the group's unity as the Strait of Hormuz blockade disrupts energy markets.

item.title: German Finance Minister Calls G7 the Right Forum to End Iran Conflict
item.description: German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil says the G7 is the appropriate platform to discuss ending the war in Iran, which along with the Strait of Hormuz blockade poses a serious threat to the global economy. The statement comes ahead of the G7 finance ministers meeting in Paris.
